rationale: A0 shows schtasks.exe creating a scheduled task named 'SoundIentifier' (typosquatting 'SoundIdentifier') that executes a payload via WMIC process call create targeting an Alternate Data Stream (ADS) path 'C:\Users\Public\Disk101.ini:59e9c935' — a classic ADS-based payload hiding technique used for defense evasion. EV-001 shows a second scheduled task 'Disk Diag' created moments later, set to run every 6 minutes via explorer.exe launching 'C:\Users\Public\Weber0.js', a JavaScript file in the Public folder — a common persistence and execution mechanism for malware. Both tasks are created from the same parent cmd.exe session under Administrator with High integrity, indicating a deliberate, scripted persistence installation campaign. The combination of ADS execution, recurring JS-based persistence, deceptive task names, and payloads staged in C:\Users\Public\ are strong indicators of post-exploitation activity.
